headline: BCG Reports $14.4 Billion in Revenue, Marking 22nd Consecutive Year of Growth
summary: Boston Consulting Group (BCG) has reported 7% global revenue growth, rising to $14.4 billion in 2025 from $13.5 billion in 2024, marking its 22nd consecutive year of growth. The firm expanded across all regions, reflecting client demand globally for large-scale transformation and applied AI impact.

headline: US software stocks slide as IBM, ServiceNow results reignite AI disruption fears
summary: U.S. software stocks fell in premarket trading on Thursday, following quarterly results from IBM and ServiceNow that reignited fears about ‌AI-driven disruption across the sector. International Business Machines said its revenue growth ‌slowed in the first quarter, pressured by weakness in its software business, anchored by its Red ​Hat cloud unit. Growth in the segment slowed to 11.3%, sending the Big Blue's shares 7.4% lower.

content.title: US software stocks slide as IBM, ServiceNow results reignite AI disruption fears

International Business Machines said its revenue growth ‌slowed in the first quarter, pressured by weakness in its software business, anchored by its Red ​Hat cloud unit. Growth in the segment slowed to 11.3%, sending the Big Blue's shares 7.4% lower.

headline: IBM Stock Slides Despite Q1 Earnings Beat. Here's Why.
summary: IBM stock fell Thursday after the tech giant's first-quarter earnings beat failed to offset concerns about AI threatening its software business.  The Armonk, New York-based company still expects full-year constant current revenue growth of more than 5%.  IBM Chief Financial Officer James Kavanaugh told analysts on a conference call Wednesday that the company has never raised its annual guidance following Q1, at least not during his nine years in the role.

content.title: IBM Stock Slides Despite Q1 Earnings Beat. Here's Why.
content.summary: IBM stock fell Thursday after the tech giant's first-quarter earnings beat failed to offset concerns about AI threatening its software business.  The Armonk, New York-based company still expects full-year constant current revenue growth of more than 5%.  IBM Chief Financial Officer James Kavanaugh told analysts on a conference call Wednesday that the company has never raised its annual guidance following Q1, at least not during his nine years in the role.

headline: US software firms slide after IBM, ServiceNow results spark fresh AI concerns
summary: U.S. software stocks fell in premarket trading on Thursday after quarterly results from IBM and ServiceNow showed slowing growth and revived fears ‌about AI-driven disruption across the sector. International Business Machines said its revenue growth slowed in the ‌first quarter, pressured by weakness in its software business, which includes its Red Hat cloud unit. Growth in the segment slowed ​11.3%, sending the Big Blue's shares 7.5% lower.

Growth in the segment slowed ​11.3%, sending the Big Blue's shares 7.5% lower.

headline: IBM beats Q1 expectations, but shares fall on softer software momentum
summary: Technology group IBM (NYSE:IBM) reported first-quarter results that exceeded market expectations on Wednesday, with revenue rising 6% at constant currency. Despite the solid performance, the company’s shares dropped more than 7% in premarket trading on Thursday, with analysts pointing to weaker-than-expected growth in the software division as a key concern.

content.title: IBM beats Q1 expectations, but shares fall on softer software momentum
content.summary: Technology group IBM (NYSE:IBM) reported first-quarter results that exceeded market expectations on Wednesday, with revenue rising 6% at constant currency. Despite the solid performance, the company’s shares dropped more than 7% in premarket trading on Thursday, with analysts pointing to weaker-than-expected growth in the software division as a key concern.
